title: [Feature Request] Two-column reading mode
author: Engreyight
body:
I have been really enjoying reading with this browser, but I did find one thing that could make using it a significantly better experience.

When reading on my tablet, I prefer to hold it in landscape mode, since it fits better in my hand, like a small book opened flat. However, when websites are in landscape mode, lines tend to be rather wide and harder to follow. I would also like these sites to look a bit more like an actual book in this setting.

Both of these issues can be resolved by using a two-column layout, splitting everything down the middle, with the height capped at or near the viewport height, similar to what this line of CSS does:

```css
columns: 2 / 100vh;
```

_(Note that setting column height [like this](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/columns), or with [its own property](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/column-height), is not currently supported by all engines.)_

I was wondering if it would be possible to implement something similar in this browser (maybe even by injecting a similar CSS to the reader), perhaps even with [column gaps](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/column-gap#multi-column_layout) customizable in the settings. The restrained column height combined with the existing touch scrolling feature would make it feel as close to turning a real page as such a device can get, I think, all while actually reading a web page, which would be pretty neat.

Thank you.

title: [Feature Request] - Save tab names in local cache
author: NeonTink
body: **What device and app version are you using** - Device: Release v15.15.0 - OS: 4.2-rel - EinkBro Version: Release v15.15.0 **Describe the bug** Hello, I have been having some significant issues with having multiple AO3 tabs attempting to load at the same time which I think is being caused by cloudflare. Toggling the "Load non-active tabs" setting to off has fixed this which is great, but now I can't tell which tabs are which as it shows the websites icon and then only three dots (...) instead of the tab name. It would be great to be able to keep "Load non-active tabs" and either have the browser pull the tab name from local memory, or maybe get the tab name from the website without loading the full page? **To Reproduce** 1. Toggle the "Load non-active tabs" setting to off 2. Restart device 3. Open EinkBro 4. Open tabs list ** What's the behavior on following similar browsers ** I'm unsure how other browsers handle this. **Screenshots**

title: Stuck in Settings upon returning to the app from recents
author: stacsk
body:
Please write necessary information as described below. If information is not enough, I may not have time to look into it.

**What device and app version are you using**

 - Device: Redmi Note 12
 - OS: Android 13
 - EinkBro Version [e.g. 9.7.0.   You can find the version number in Settings.]15.15.0

**Describe the bug**
When I open the settings and go into the "recent apps" (which shows the currently running apps), the app title changes to "SettingActivity" and pressing "back" (by either device button or app's back button) kicks me out of the app.
**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior.

Example description:
1. Go to Settings
2. Click on your device's "recent apps" button
3. Select EinkBro, which is titled "SettingActivity"
4. Press back in order to exit Settings
5. Observe that you're kicked out of the app

** What's the behavior on following similar browsers **
* Via Browser
* xBrowser
I don't know.
**Do not report that it works in CHROME, FIREFOX, or EDGE, BRAVE. That does not help. Since these are full-fledge browsers**
  
**Expected behavior**
Pressing back should take me back to my tabs.
**Screenshots**
![Screenshot_2026-07-02-00-32-38-655_com.miui.home-edit.jpg](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e22cc70c-4682-4fd3-81e7-db0b1c027e01)


If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Additional context**

Add any other context about the problem here.

body: - Device: Bigme hibreak Color - OS: Android 12 - EinkBro Version [15.15.0] **Describe the bug** Can't modify the name of a photo saved. When the prompt comes (after a long press on the picture on a webpage), i cannot open the keyboard to type/modifiy the name. SUGGESTION --> Is it possible to add the option to choose where the downloads are saved + modifiy the name before starting de the download itself. Thanks a lot for the work, I like this useful and fast browser !!! Best regards Antou

title: Cloudflare verification failing
author: cdmoldes
body:
This has been a pretty peristent issue lately - any cloudflare verification fails or loops endlessly, for example, at: https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/02685809211068664

I've disabled adblock, tried on both mobile data and wifi, and cannot proceed. I tried a different browser and was able to successfully pass verification.

title: [Feture Request] will this browser support extension?
author: Coucou1818
body: it will be perfect if it can support extensions

title: [Feature Request] Support for Tampermonkey / Userscripts
author: asanokiyoe
body:
Thank you for developing such a great browser! 
I recently started using this browser on an E-ink device, and the experience has been very positive so far.
I noticed that the browser already supports "Custom CSS" and "Post-load JavaScript" features. I was wondering if there are any plans to support Tampermonkey (userscripts) in the future?
I have a collection of frequently used Tampermonkey scripts that I rely on daily. In my previous browsers that supported userscripts, these tools significantly improved my browsing efficiency. Having this capability on an E-ink optimized browser would be incredibly helpful.
Thank you for considering this request, and for your time and effort in developing this browser.

title: Request Hard Reload
author: MichaelaMer
body: Hello - we are a volunteer EMS agency and are experimenting with documentation on an older a Boox. It kind of looks promising, but we can't find a way to HARD reload Javascript during the development process (like Shift+Reload on other web-browsers).  This makes it very awkward to develop a webapp for einkbro / on a eink-tablet. Any suggestions will be appreciated.

title: Layer type settings option
author: Apaaja20
body:
Allows users to control WebView hardware acceleration.
Thus notably turning WebGL on and off.
Also useful on platforms where hardware acceleration is not working reliably.
For instance, WSA on Windows 11.

title: Disable history sugestions when clicking address bar
author: aadcg
body:
I've disabled history settings, selected "Clear History" and deleted the database. However, when I click the address bar, I still see lots of URLs.

Version 15.14.0 (from F-Droid).

author: Apaaja20

title: Feature Request: button for sharing html of page
author: smartscripts-nl
body:
As it is now, I only can share the url of a page. It would be nice if we could share the html of the page.

I use Joplin to save articles, and a button to share the page content to Joplin would be really handy.

title: Feature Request: toggle Reader Mode with a gesture.
author: smartscripts-nl
body: As it is now, I have to use the menu to enable or disable Reader Mode. It would be great if it could be toggled with a gesture.

title: Feature Request: option to show images in Reader Mode
author: smartscripts-nl
body: Currently it is possible to Keep Extra Content for Reader Mode. It would be very nice if a similar option existed for images: so to enable plain Reader Mode, without the Extra Content, but _with_ images. That would come in handy for reading my online newspaper.

title: Save as MHT
author: ge634510
body:
Seems to be missing in the latest version. Has it been removed? Hope not because saving as PDF or Epub literally doesn't work for some sites. The content in the  saved pages in these formats are either screwed up, missing images or other things or just plain unreadable.

Saving as MHT is great if you want to save something and want to view it later on a PC running Windows or Linux after saving it.

A save option similar to the one Save as MHT used here would basically cover everything one could run into, and would let you save the file where you wanted like on a external SD card.

title: [Bug/Feature Request] App ignores system rotation lock / Request for an in-app orientation lock button
author: chunsal
body:
**Describe the bug / feature request**
Hi, I am using EinkBro on my tablet. Currently, I have my system's auto-rotate feature turned OFF and locked to Landscape mode. However, EinkBro ignores the system-level rotation lock and continues to rotate automatically when I move the device. 

**Expected behavior**
The browser should respect the Android system's auto-rotate settings. If the system is locked to landscape or portrait, the browser should not rotate.

**Proposed Solution**
1. **Sync with System:** Make the app respect the system-level orientation lock settings.
2. **In-app Toggle:** Add a dedicated "Orientation Lock" button in the toolbar or a toggle in the settings (e.g., Force Landscape / Force Portrait / Auto) for better user control. 

**Device information:**
- Device Model: SM-T285
- Android Version: 7.1.2
- EinkBro Version: Latest

Thank you for your great work on this browser!
